canonical_name: WP1 formal identifiability & symmetry theorem (relabeling-invariance scope audit)
research_question: Is the prior campaign's sign-relabeling result a GLOBAL stopping theorem proving internal methods can never touch the value layer?
claim_layer: L2 (structure/identifiability of the C/V partition); formal meta-claim about method power, no reading
method_family: formal proof / group-theoretic identifiability analysis
scientific_outcome: REFUTED (of the prior global theorem) / STRUCTURAL_ONLY
data_sources_corpus_version: N/A (formal argument); illustrated on silver LA + DAMOS LB (as_of 2026-07-07)
source_dependencies: prior campaign relabeling-invariance result (linear_a_campaign); Ventris-Chadwick C/V value structure for the target-space model
effective_n_independence_unit: NOT_APPLICABLE (theorem); illustration uses signs as units
features_channels: abstract objective classes O_id (identity-cooccurrence) vs O_rel (position/substitution/morphology coupled to value-space)
algorithm_model: group theory: Sym(S) orbit invariance under O_id; residual subgroup S_v x S_(n-v) under O_rel
search_space_params: |S|~92 syllabograms; symmetry group order 92! collapses to 5!*87! under a C/V-coupled objective
preregistration_plan_hash: NOT_RECORDED (WP1 not individually preregistered; CAMPAIGN.md quota framing)
heldout_design: N/A (theorem); empirical leg validated on known-truth LB (FND-02)
positive_negative_controls: positive control = known-truth LB where the C/V partition is knowable
primary_metric_value: symmetry-group order reduction 92! -> 5!*87! (~1e9 x smaller) [REPORT_ONLY, illustrative]
power: N/A
ablations_robustness: paired with empirical counterexample FND-02 and multi-feature FND-04
contamination_leakage: none (no LA values used); LB used only as illustrative known-truth
strongest_authorized_claim: The relabeling invariance holds ONLY for identity-cooccurrence objectives; objectives coupling internal relational structure to value structure break the C/V symmetry, so the value layer is not provably closed to internal methods.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That internal evidence yields any actual phonetic reading, or that the C/V partition is calibrated on LA.
limitations_blockers: Reduces equivalence-class SIZE only; still needs an external anchor per residual class to fix absolute values.
supersession_relations: SUPERSEDES prior campaign's 'internal methods value-blind forever' claim (correction C1). The downstream position->C/V empirical leg it motivates was LATER REFUTED as a frequency artifact by the relative-phonology campaign.
verdict_ledger_reference: DECISION_LOG.md 2026-07-08 WP1; FINAL_CAMPAIGN_REPORT §2 C1; CAMPAIGN WP-ledger row 1

canonical_name: WP1 word-initial-rate position statistic — C/V symmetry-break counterexample
research_question: Does a single word-position feature separate vowels from consonants above chance (LB known-truth), and surface vowel candidates on LA non-circularly?
method_family: distributional position statistic + permutation test
scientific_outcome: PARTIAL_SUPPORT (LB, single-feature-modest) / RELATIVE_PHONOLOGY_ONLY on LA (candidate ranking)
data_sources_corpus_version: DAMOS Linear B (values known) for validation; silver LA packed inscriptions for application; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: AB homomorphy (GORILA/Ventris) used only as a non-training circular check on the LA vowel candidates
effective_n_independence_unit: LB: 77 signs freq>=20 (5 true vowels present); LA: 46 signs freq>=20; unit = sign type
features_channels: word-initial-rate (single position feature)
algorithm_model: rank signs by initial-rate; AUC vowels-vs-rest; permutation null
heldout_design: known-truth LB validation gates the LA application; LA vowel identity is a post-hoc circular check, not training
positive_negative_controls: positive = LB vowels {A,E,I,O,U}; permutation null = negative
primary_metric_value: LB vowel-vs-consonant AUC = 0.744, permutation p = 0.035 [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp1_symmetry_break.json]; LA top initial-rate signs A(0.43)/I(0.20)/U(0.18) [VERIFIED]
uncertainty: single-feature, modest; true LB vowel ranks by initial-rate A=4,E=9,O=14,I=29,U=51
power: single feature; strengthened by multi-feature WP3.1
ablations_robustness: multi-feature extension in FND-04
contamination_leakage: non-circular on LA (no LB values as inputs); LA vowel check acknowledged as circular
strongest_authorized_claim: Word-position is a relabeling-variant signal separating LB vowels from consonants (AUC 0.744, p=0.035); LA's own position structure ranks A/I/U as vowel-like.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That A/I/U are read as vowels on LA (calibration fails; ranking only).
limitations_blockers: single-feature-modest; LA yields a ranking not a calibrated partition.
supersession_relations: LATER REFUTED: the position->C/V signal was shown by the relative-phonology campaign to be a frequency artifact (per task note). Feeds FND-04, FND-13.

canonical_name: WP1 symmetry-breaking atlas (13-channel enumeration)
research_question: Which channels can, in principle, reduce the sign-value equivalence classes, and how do they rank by leverage/circularity?
method_family: taxonomy / design enumeration
implementation_status: PROPOSED_ONLY (design catalogue; individual channels implemented in later WPs)
scientific_outcome: STRUCTURAL_ONLY (organizing scaffold)
data_sources_corpus_version: N/A
source_dependencies: cross-script scholarship (Salgarella homomorphy, S&M Cypriot) named as circular/dependent channels
effective_n_independence_unit: 13 channels catalogued
features_channels: POSITION, SCRIBAL_SUBSTITUTION, ORTHOGRAPHIC_ALTERNATION, MORPHOLOGY, PHONOTACTICS, SIGN_SHAPE_ALLOGRAPHY, EXTERNAL_ANCHORS, BILINGUAL, CROSS_SCRIPT_AB_SHARED, CRETAN_HIEROGLYPHIC, CYPRO_MINOAN, ACROPHONY_PICTORIAL, MEASURE_CORRESPONDENCE
algorithm_model: qualitative leverage/circularity grading
heldout_design: N/A
positive_negative_controls: N/A
primary_metric_value: 13 channels; >=5 independent INTERNAL relative channels identified [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/symmetry_breaking_atlas.json]
power: N/A
ablations_robustness: N/A
strongest_authorized_claim: There exist >=5 independent internal relative channels (vs the prior campaign's recognition of only bilingual+anchors) that could reduce equivalence-class size.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That any listed channel yields an LA reading.
limitations_blockers: a design catalogue, not a run; power realized only in WP3/4/5.
supersession_relations: scaffolds FND-04..FND-18

canonical_name: WP3.1 multi-feature C/V classifier (logistic, LB 7-fold CV) + frequency ablation
research_question: Can a multi-feature distributional classifier recover the C/V partition on known-truth LB, and does it transfer to LA?
method_family: supervised logistic classifier + permutation null + feature ablation
scientific_outcome: SUPPORTED on LB (partition recovered) / RELATIVE_PHONOLOGY_ONLY (ranking, not calibrated partition) on LA
data_sources_corpus_version: DAMOS Linear B (values) for train/CV; silver LA for application; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: LB known values (grading only); typological frequency prior (external linguistic knowledge)
effective_n_independence_unit: LB 74 signs (5 vowels); LA 46 signs; unit = sign type; 7-fold CV
features_channels: initial_rate, final_rate, mean_pos, lone_rate, lnbr_ent, rnbr_ent, log_freq
algorithm_model: logistic regression, 7-fold CV, 200 permutation nulls; ablation by dropping/isolating features
heldout_design: 7-fold CV on LB; LA is out-of-domain application (no LA labels)
positive_negative_controls: positive = LB C/V truth; negative = permutation null
multiplicity_correction: single CV AUC test + reported ablations (not FWER-adjusted)
primary_metric_value: LB 7-fold CV AUC = 0.835 (perm p=0.01); log_freq-only AUC = 0.838; position-only AUC = 0.67; minus-log_freq = 0.783 [ALL VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp3_cv_recovery.json]
power: adequate on LB; LA probabilities all <0.05 (domain shift, no calibration)
ablations_robustness: KEY ablation: the strong 0.835 signal is the typological frequency prior (0.838), NOT corpus structure; genuine corpus-structural symmetry-break is position-only AUC 0.67 (modest)
contamination_leakage: non-circular on LA inputs; frequency prior is external typological knowledge (labelled)
strongest_authorized_claim: A C/V partition is recoverable on LB (AUC 0.835), but the strong channel is a typological frequency prior; genuine corpus-structural evidence is modest (0.67). LA yields a candidate ranking (A,I top), not a calibrated partition.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That the LA C/V partition is established, or that A/I are read as vowels.
limitations_blockers: LB->LA domain shift blocks probability calibration; frequency confound means much of the 'signal' is prior, not corpus.
supersession_relations: LATER REFUTED: position->C/V corpus signal shown to be a frequency artifact by the relative-phonology campaign (task note). Ablation here already flagged frequency dominance.

canonical_name: WP3.1b unsupervised LA-internal C/V clustering (k-means/GMM)
research_question: Can 2-class unsupervised clustering of the position features recover C/V without any labels (on LB, gating LA)?
method_family: unsupervised clustering + ARI permutation null (fail-closed gate)
scientific_outcome: NULL (needs labels)
data_sources_corpus_version: DAMOS LB benchmark (gate); silver LA withheld (fail-closed); as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: LB truth for ARI grading only
effective_n_independence_unit: LB ~77 signs; unit = sign type
features_channels: standardized WP3.1 position features
algorithm_model: k-means and GMM, 2 classes; ARI vs 500-realization label-permutation null
heldout_design: LB known-truth gate BLOCKS LA application (fail-closed)
positive_negative_controls: positive would be supervised WP3.1 (0.835); here at chance
primary_metric_value: LB k-means ARI=0.0034 (perm p=0.4571), GMM ARI=0.0062 — at chance; vowel purity ~0.09 [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp3_cv_cluster.json]
power: dominant variance axis (frequency) is orthogonal to the 5-vs-69 C/V split
ablations_robustness: both k-means and GMM fail identically
strongest_authorized_claim: Unsupervised clustering CANNOT recover C/V; a few correct seed labels are required (motivates WP5 bootstrap).
forbidden_stronger_claim: Any LA C/V clustering result (withheld, fail-closed).
limitations_blockers: class imbalance (5 vs 69) + frequency-dominated variance defeat unsupervised recovery.
supersession_relations: motivates FND-13 (reduced-seed bootstrap)
verdict_ledger_reference: DECISION_LOG 2026-07-08 WP3 core; WP3 report sub-3.1b

canonical_name: WP3.2 scribal-substitution similarity graph (n-gram context, degree-preserving null)
research_question: Do strong scribal-substitution edges recover phonological similarity (same-C/same-V) on LB, and does LA reach the clean regime?
method_family: substitution-context graph + threshold sweep vs degree-preserving null
scientific_outcome: SUPPORTED on LB (SIGNAL_VALIDATED) / NO_POWER on LA (underpowered)
data_sources_corpus_version: DAMOS LB (benchmark) + silver LA packed inscriptions; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: LB values grade the LB benchmark only
effective_n_independence_unit: LB graded subgraph 72 nodes/2538 edges; LA 539 sequences; unit = sign-pair edge
features_channels: 2/3/4-gram substitution contexts; edge WEIGHT (not existence); hub-normalized weight
algorithm_model: substitution graph, threshold sweep, degree-preserving (configuration-model) null, weight->feature AUC
heldout_design: LB known-truth grades benchmark; LA application not calibratable (underpowered)
positive_negative_controls: positive = LB same-C/V recovery; negative = degree-preserving null
multiplicity_correction: threshold sweep reported; signal in weight
primary_metric_value: LB same-feature recovery 1.39x null, z=7.07, hub-norm weight->feature AUC=0.714 (raw-weight 0.552); base_rate 0.2477; LA max edge weight=105 vs LB 303 [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp3_2_scribal_substitution.json]. Note z reported as 7.07 in report; sweep table p=0.0033.
power: LA max weight 105 below the ~120 clean regime
ablations_robustness: near-complete unthresholded graph (density ~0.99) shown uninformative; signal isolated to weight
strongest_authorized_claim: Scribal substitution is an independent relational channel recovering phonological similarity on LB (z=7.07, AUC 0.714); LA is underpowered for it.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That the LA substitution subgraph yields any similarity reading.
limitations_blockers: LA max-weight 105 (packed) below clean regime; segmentation makes it worse (FND-17).
supersession_relations: power quantified in FND-07; word-segmented variant FND-17 (hurts)
record_confidence: VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT (z=7.07 is report-level; sweep table shows p=0.0033 — minor presentational gap)

canonical_name: WP3.2b substitution power curve (LB subsample ladder)
research_question: How far below the validated substitution clean regime is LA, in corpus-size terms?
method_family: corpus-size learning curve (subsampling)
scientific_outcome: PARTIAL_SUPPORT / QUANTIFIED (LA ~1.7x below clean regime, not at a floor)
data_sources_corpus_version: DAMOS LB subsampled 1270..13562 wordforms; LA operating point; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: LB grading; shares substitution channel with FND-06
effective_n_independence_unit: 6 corpus sizes; unit = subsampled corpus
features_channels: max edge weight + weight->feature AUC vs corpus size
algorithm_model: subsample LB, recompute substitution graph, track max-weight and AUC
heldout_design: N/A (characterization)
positive_negative_controls: LB full-corpus = clean regime reference
primary_metric_value: max-weight vs size: 3000w->112, 5000w->150, 8000w->212, 13562w->303; LA(539 seq)=105; clean regime (>=120) at ~5000 LB words; LA-equivalent ~3000 => corpus_multiple_needed=1.7 [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp3_2b_power_curve.json]
uncertainty: single subsample per size; AUC weak (0.565-0.579 raw)
power: explicitly a power characterization
ablations_robustness: monotone growth of signal with size
strongest_authorized_claim: LA sits ~1.7x below the substitution clean regime — below the knee, not at a floor.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That 1.7x more corpus WOULD yield a reading (only that the channel would reach validated power).
limitations_blockers: CONFLICT-of-magnitude: the substitution channel says ~1.7x while the seed-propagation channel (FND-14) says ~4.9x — different channels, not reconciled to a single multiple.
supersession_relations: refines correction C2; contrast FND-14 (4.9x)

canonical_name: WP3.3 orthographic-alternation detector
research_question: Do recurrent minimal-pair alternations recover inflectional same-C/same-V structure (LB), and does LA have the support?
method_family: minimal-pair alternation detector + corpus-shuffle null
scientific_outcome: SUPPORTED on LB / NO_POWER on LA (data-limited)
data_sources_corpus_version: DAMOS LB 13562 wordforms; silver LA (unsegmented, inscription=unit); as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: LB (C,V) parse for grading only
effective_n_independence_unit: LB 1740 recurrent pairs (support>=3); LA only 1 pair reaches support>=3
features_channels: sign-identity + position minimal pairs; same-V / same-C recovery rate
algorithm_model: detector + 300-realization corpus-shuffle null
heldout_design: LB known-truth grades benchmark
positive_negative_controls: positive = Greek inflectional alternations on LB; negative = shuffle null
primary_metric_value: LB sameV enrichment 1.15x (p=0.0033), sameC 1.452x (p=0.0033); LA only 1 pair support>=3 => NO_POWER [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp3_3_orthographic_alternations.json]
power: LA unsegmented -> no support
ablations_robustness: top alternation pairs (JA/JO, TA/TO) are same-C consistent with Greek
strongest_authorized_claim: The alternation channel fires on LB but LA lacks the segmented support to run it.
forbidden_stronger_claim: Any LA morphophonological reading.
limitations_blockers: requires word segmentation LA does not have in the packed regime.
supersession_relations: part of WP3 core VALIDATED(LB)/UNDERPOWERED(LA) pattern
verdict_ledger_reference: DECISION_LOG 2026-07-08 WP3 core; WP3 report sub-3.3

canonical_name: WP3.4 morphology C/V-typing detector (bits/word gain)
research_question: Does a morphology/typed-bigram model add predictive information (LB), and does LA have productive paradigms?
method_family: typed-bigram information-gain detector + held-out-by-stem CV + permutation null
scientific_outcome: SUPPORTED on LB (detector validated) / NULL on LA (recurrence artifact)
data_sources_corpus_version: DAMOS LB 13562 wordforms (704 stems / 77 signs); silver LA; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: LB oracle typing for benchmark
effective_n_independence_unit: 5-fold, held out by lexical family = word[:-1] (paradigm relatives held together)
features_channels: morph-bigram vs untyped/typed bits-per-word
algorithm_model: smoothed typed/untyped bigram LMs; 300 perm nulls; held-out-by-stem
heldout_design: paradigm-relative held-out to avoid wordform-recurrence leakage
positive_negative_controls: positive = LB morphology; negative = permutation + the LA recurrence check
primary_metric_value: LB G_morph = +0.7309 bits/word (bigram 20.5374 vs untyped 19.8065); LA gain = whole-wordform-recurrence artifact (no productive paradigms) => NULL [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp3_4_morphology_cv.json]
power: LA lacks paradigm depth
ablations_robustness: typing itself adds nothing over untyped (G_typing = -0.0229) — the gain is morphology, not the C/V typing
contamination_leakage: recurrence artifact explicitly diagnosed and excluded on LA
strongest_authorized_claim: The morphology detector is valid on LB (+0.731 bits/word) but LA's apparent gain is a wordform-recurrence artifact, not productive morphology.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That LA has recovered morphological paradigms.
limitations_blockers: short LA words; no held-out paradigm structure.
supersession_relations: consistent with the standing morphology NO-POWER null in the frozen paper
verdict_ledger_reference: DECISION_LOG 2026-07-08 WP3 core; WP3 report sub-3.4

canonical_name: WP2 segmentation lever (packed vs GORILA word units, C/V vowel recovery)
research_question: Does using GORILA WORD units (vs packed inscriptions) improve LA vowel recovery through the LB-trained C/V classifier?
method_family: text-unit ablation (segmentation encoding comparison)
scientific_outcome: PARTIAL_SUPPORT (segmentation helps this channel; a methodological-artifact correction)
data_sources_corpus_version: silver LA: 539 packed inscriptions vs 3147 GORILA word units; LB-trained classifier; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: LB-trained C/V classifier (FND-04); LA vowel identities as non-circular check only
effective_n_independence_unit: packed 46 signs vs word-seg 60 signs; unit = sign type; 5 known vowels as check
features_channels: word-position stats under two text-unit encodings
algorithm_model: apply LB C/V classifier to each encoding; AUC vowels-vs-rest
segmentation: THE variable: packed inscriptions (mean 7.88 signs) vs GORILA words (mean 1.84)
heldout_design: LA vowel identity is the check, not a model input
positive_negative_controls: LB CV ceiling 0.835 as reference
primary_metric_value: LA vowel-vs-rest AUC 0.685 (packed) -> 0.760 (word-segmented), toward LB ceiling 0.835 [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp2_segmentation_lever.json]
power: shows part of the 'underpowered' obstacle is a text-unit artifact, correctable now
ablations_robustness: contrasted with substitution channel where segmentation HURTS (FND-17) => channel-dependent
strongest_authorized_claim: Using GORILA word units lifts LA vowel recovery 0.685->0.760 — a methodological artifact (wrong text unit), correctable without new excavation.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That the LA C/V partition is now solved, or that segmentation uniformly helps.
limitations_blockers: helps position/C/V but hurts substitution — no single best encoding.
supersession_relations: LATER REFUTED: position->C/V recovery is a frequency artifact (task note); the segmentation gain rides on that same channel. Channel-dependence corrected by FND-17.

canonical_name: WP4 diachronic & cross-script sign-evolution (shape / distributional / chronology channels)
research_question: Can cross-script sign evolution (LA->LB->Cypriot) transfer value non-circularly?
method_family: cross-script alignment (Procrustes/MUSE) + leave-one-sign-out + shape-channel Fisher test
scientific_outcome: NULL (non-circular distributional channel) / CIRCULAR (shape channel capped <=0.75)
mechanical_verdict: NULL (4th distributional null); shared-AB hypothesis REFUTED non-circularly
data_sources_corpus_version: silver LA + DAMOS LB + Cypriot (S&M 2017) + Salgarella 2020 homomorphy grades; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: Salgarella homomorphy = LB identity (circular); S&M Cypriot stability; shares the same 'core stable syllabogram' latent
effective_n_independence_unit: distributional LOTO: 56 signs vs 89 LB partners; unit = sign
features_channels: shape(homomorphy), admin-distributional co-occurrence, chronology — kept separate
algorithm_model: Procrustes/MUSE alignment; rank true LB partner; shuffled-anchor 10-perm null; 2x2 Fisher for shape
preregistration_plan_hash: one of two frozen prereg one-shots referenced (Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.21168887) — NOT re-run here
heldout_design: leave-one-sign-out; shuffled-anchor null
positive_negative_controls: positive = shape recovers LB<->Cypriot stable-11; negative = shuffled-anchor null
null_family_run_count: shuffled-anchor null (10-perm) — the 4th independent distributional null in the lineage
primary_metric_value: distributional R@1=0.000, R@5=0.071 vs null 0.066 (perm-p 0.50-0.60), median true-partner rank 48/89; shape stable-11 recall 11/11, precision 0.44, Fisher one-sided p=1.59e-5 but CONFOUNDED [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp4_calibration.json]
power: distributional at chance floor
ablations_robustness: three channels separated to isolate the confound
contamination_leakage: shape->LB-value is circular (recall@1=1.0 by construction; <=0.75 cap)
strongest_authorized_claim: No non-circular cross-script value constraint exists; the shape channel that 'works' is convergent-confounded, not independent.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That LA sign values transfer from LB/Cypriot by shape.
limitations_blockers: the only firing channel (shape) is circular; distributional channel is powerless.
supersession_relations: consistent with the standing cross-script REFUTE_LOTO_FRAGILE / distributional-null lineage (memory: crossscript-gate)

canonical_name: WP5(a) external-anchor inventory (3-channel provenance census)
research_question: How many LA signs carry a value anchor, and how many survive held-out (independent of nominal multi-channel counts)?
method_family: provenance census + dependency-tier reconciliation
scientific_outcome: STRUCTURAL_ONLY / TRIVIAL_RECOVERY (only 2 held-out pins survive independently)
data_sources_corpus_version: crossscript_gate anchors.csv / anchor_census.csv / toponym_anchors.csv / salgarella_2020_grades.json (sibling worktree logos-la-lb-continuity)
source_dependencies: GORILA/Ventris value lineage; Salgarella homomorphy; S&M Cypriot; toponym equations
effective_n_independence_unit: 115 provenance records / 62 signs; unit = sign; held-out survivors = 2 (I, RI) per the frozen gate
features_channels: L lexical/onomastic, H homomorphy, C Cypriot stability
algorithm_model: record aggregation + dependency-tier classification (one-toponym-deep / multi-channel / structural-only)
heldout_design: the frozen cross-script gate recovered only 2 held-out anchors, exposing nominal-independence inflation
positive_negative_controls: N/A
multiplicity_correction: dependency-tier reconciliation (nominal 26 multi-channel vs 2 held-out survivable)
primary_metric_value: 115 records / 62 signs; 47 lexical / 57 homomorphy / 11 Cypriot; 26 nominal multi-channel-independent but only 2 held-out (I,RI); 3 one-toponym-deep pins; 31 structural-only [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp5_anchor_inventory.json; note the JSON summary lists 26 'held_out_survivable' which the REPORT overrides down to 2 via the frozen gate — CONFLICTING]
power: anchors are not the bottleneck (A,I,U exist as seeds)
ablations_robustness: cross-checked against WP5b bootstrap
contamination_leakage: single point of failure: every phonetic anchor inherits GORILA homomorphic transfer
strongest_authorized_claim: Only ~2-3 genuinely held-out one-deep value pins exist; nominal multi-channel counts (26) overstate independence ~13x.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That 26 signs have independent held-out values.
limitations_blockers: CONFLICTING record: JSON summary field n_held_out_survivable_candidate=26 vs report/gate=2 — the report explicitly de-rates the JSON.
supersession_relations: feeds FND-13 seed choice

canonical_name: WP5(b) reduced-seed C/V bootstrap (semi-supervised label spreading)
research_question: Can a few correct C/V seeds propagate over the position+substitution affinity graph to recover the partition (LB), and does it transfer to LA?
method_family: semi-supervised label spreading over affinity graph
scientific_outcome: SUPPORTED on LB (mechanism validated) / NULL on LA (propagation power-limited)
data_sources_corpus_version: DAMOS LB (grade) + silver LA; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: WP3.1 position + WP3.2 substitution graphs; frequency only picks seeds
effective_n_independence_unit: LB 74 signs / 5 vowels; held-out AUC over seed combos; LA 46 signs / 4 vowels
features_channels: position + substitution affinity (log_freq excluded from propagation)
algorithm_model: label spreading (alpha=0.2, knn=7, beta_sub=0.5); kv=3,4 vowel seeds + 3 consonant seeds
heldout_design: held-out vowel AUC on LB; seeds excluded from scoring
positive_negative_controls: positive = clean seeds; negative = frequency-seed null + random-seed null + LA transfer null
primary_metric_value: unsupervised floor AUC 0.568 -> 3-4 correct seeds 0.78 (kv=3) -> 0.82/0.87 (kv=4 pos/pos+sub) ~ supervised ceiling 0.835; frequency-seeding fails (peaks 0.726, p=0.13-0.25, top-freq are consonants TO/JO/KO); LA transfer NULL (held-out ~0.01-0.016, U buried) [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp5_reduced_seed_bootstrap.json]
power: LA affinity graph too weak to propagate seeds
ablations_robustness: two honest nulls: frequency-seeds fail; LA transfer fails
contamination_leakage: non-circular (LB grades only; A/I seeds transcription-definitional)
strongest_authorized_claim: On LB, 3-4 CORRECT seeds propagate to recover C/V; on LA the same propagation is null (power-limited), and frequency-seeds do not substitute for correct seeds.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That LA C/V is recoverable from available anchors now.
limitations_blockers: LA internal propagation power; needs correct (not frequent) seeds.
supersession_relations: LATER REFUTED: seed-propagation shown to be a frequency artifact by the relative-phonology campaign (task note). Power quantified in FND-14; word-seg variant FND-18.

canonical_name: WP5.2 seed-propagation power curve (LB size ladder)
research_question: At what corpus size does the reduced-seed C/V bootstrap cross AUC 0.70, and how far below is LA?
method_family: corpus-size learning curve for the seed bootstrap
scientific_outcome: QUANTIFIED (LA ~chance; needs ~4.9x corpus, lower bound)
data_sources_corpus_version: DAMOS LB subsampled 300..13562 wordforms; LA 539; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: shares bootstrap with FND-13
effective_n_independence_unit: 10 sizes x 24 subsamples x seed combos; unit = subsample
features_channels: held-out C/V AUC vs corpus size (bar_auc=0.70)
algorithm_model: subsample LB, run WP5 bootstrap (3 vowel + 3 consonant seeds), track mean held-out AUC + CI
heldout_design: held-out vowel AUC per size
positive_negative_controls: LB full-size = clean reference
null_family_run_count: 24 subsamples per size (variance), no separate perm null
primary_metric_value: AUC crosses 0.70 at ~2620 wf (bracket 2000-3500); monotone 0.647->0.752 over 539->13562; LA(539) ~chance => needs ~4.9x corpus (bracket 3.7-6.5x, lower bound) [REPORT_ONLY headline; data curve shows size 539 kv=3 AUC 0.647 but size 300 kv=3 AUC 0.7747 — noisy/non-monotone at the low end, CONFLICTING with the smooth-monotone framing]
uncertainty: CI95 reported per size (e.g. 539 kv=3: 0.617-0.675); low-end non-monotone (300>539)
power: the WP5 LA null is corpus-starvation not a broken detector
ablations_robustness: 24 subsamples per size
strongest_authorized_claim: LA needs ~4.9x its corpus (lower bound) for the C/V bootstrap to fire; the null is starvation, not a dead detector.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That 4.9x more corpus yields a reading.
limitations_blockers: CONFLICTS with FND-07's ~1.7x (substitution channel); the low-end curve is non-monotone (size 300 AUC 0.775 > size 539 AUC 0.647), so the '~4.9x' point estimate is noisy.
supersession_relations: LATER REFUTED (seed-propagation = frequency artifact, task note); contrast FND-07
record_confidence: CONFLICTING (headline 4.9x vs non-monotone low-end curve; vs FND-07 1.7x)

canonical_name: Synthetic recovery lab (planted CV-syllabary, 5 regimes)
research_question: Do the channels recover planted phonology at scale, calibrate to NO_POWER at LA-scale, and reject wrong-language — proving LA nulls are power-limited not method-dead?
method_family: generative synthetic corpus + full-pipeline recovery across regimes
scientific_outcome: SUPPORTED (methods proven power-limited, not method-dead) / QUANTIFIED 5/5
data_sources_corpus_version: synthetic CV-syllabary calibrated to real LA/LB moments; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: real LA/LB summary stats as calibration targets
effective_n_independence_unit: 5 regimes; 200 reps; unit = synthetic corpus
features_channels: position, substitution, WP5 bootstrap under synthetic truth
algorithm_model: generative model (soft planted onset-vowel bias + inflectional endings) + the WP1/3/5 detectors
search_space_params: planted config (15 cons/5 vowel/1400 roots/zipf 0.75); scale sweep to 43868 tokens; 200 reps
heldout_design: synthetic ground truth = the check
positive_negative_controls: positive = LB-scale word-seg recovery; calibrated-failure = same language LA-scale packed; wrong-language = negative
primary_metric_value: (1) LB-scale positive: position AUC 0.778, substitution fires 100% (z=3.72), WP5 0.677; (2) LA-scale packed same-language: position AUC 0.536 (CI straddles chance), substitution 0%, WP5 0.548 = NO_POWER; (3) wrong-language ~chance at every scale; recovery (AUC>=0.75) needs >=8000 tokens word-seg ~1.88x LA; LA-packed never clears 0.75 (segmentation tax) [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp6_synthetic_recovery_lab.json]
uncertainty: CI reported; calibrated so synthetic-LB ~0.83 (not trivial 1.0)
power: the core deliverable: LA nulls are corpus-power-limited, not method failures
ablations_robustness: 5 regimes incl. wrong-language and adversarial=false calibration
strongest_authorized_claim: The machinery recovers planted signal at scale, refuses to hallucinate it when underpowered, and rejects wrong-language — so LA's empirical nulls are corpus-power-limited.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That LA specifically will recover at 1.88x (synthetic, not LA).
limitations_blockers: generalizes from synthetic planted phonology, not LA itself.
supersession_relations: underwrites the 'not method-dead' framing across WP3/5

canonical_name: WP6 agnostic reduced-K-class value search + multi-family end-to-end null
research_question: Does any agnostic reduced-K value map on real LA beat the decisive value-specific nulls (wrong-language LB + random-prior)?
method_family: beam-search value assignment + multi-family end-to-end null (S/W/R)
scientific_outcome: NULL (fails decisive value-specific nulls)
data_sources_corpus_version: silver LA (46 freq>=20 signs) + DAMOS LB as wrong-language null; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: WP3.1 C/V prior + WP3.2 substitution steering (recomputed LA-internally); NO LB value as LA input
effective_n_independence_unit: 4 folds; K in {2,4,6,8}; unit = held-out token
features_channels: held-out class-bigram likelihood improvement (nats/token) + substitution + C/V priors
algorithm_model: beam search (width 5, 8 iterations), steering coefficients fixed
search_space_params: K selected on train; fixed beam/iters/penalty; 3 null families x n=3 each
preregistration_plan_hash: NOT_RECORDED (fixed coeffs pre-set in design block)
heldout_design: held-out per-token predictive improvement; S/W/R nulls
positive_negative_controls: S order-shuffle (structure control), W wrong-language LB, R random-prior (decisive)
primary_metric_value: real gain +0.01339 nats/tok; beats S order-shuffle (z=+8.46, mean -0.0369) BUT W wrong-language LB scores higher (FWER_W=1.0, z=-2.091) and R random-prior sits at real (FWER_R~0.5); pooled decisive FWER=0.71 [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp6_agnostic_search_endtoend_null.json]
power: detects generic linguistic order, not a value map (necessary-not-sufficient)
ablations_robustness: multi-family null separates 'has sequential structure' from 'has a value map'
strongest_authorized_claim: No agnostic value map beats wrong-language LB or a random prior; LA has real sequential structure but the objective cannot recover a value map.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That any K-class value assignment is a partial reading.
limitations_blockers: small null n (3/family); the objective detects order, not values.
supersession_relations: replicated by candidate rounds FND-19/20/21

canonical_name: WP7 word-segmented substitution channel (segmentation trade-off)
research_question: Does GORILA word segmentation help or hurt the substitution channel (vs the packed regime)?
method_family: text-unit ablation of the substitution graph
scientific_outcome: REFUTED (of the 'segmentation is a uniform win' claim) / NULL for this channel word-segmented
mechanical_verdict: AT_END_TO_END_NULL for the word-segmented substitution channel (opaque-LB gate still fires)
data_sources_corpus_version: silver LA GORILA word units (2479 filtered) vs packed 539; DAMOS LB gate; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: shares channel with FND-06
effective_n_independence_unit: word units mean 1.84-2.0 signs; unit = sign-pair edge
features_channels: substitution context weight under word segmentation
algorithm_model: substitution graph + degree-preserving null on word units; opaque-LB gate
heldout_design: opaque-LB gate (word units) still fires z=8.46
positive_negative_controls: opaque-LB word-unit gate = positive control (fires); LA = null
primary_metric_value: LA max context-weight 105 (packed) -> 47 (word); strong edges 328->154; C/V-boundary enrichment z 2.59 -> -0.50 (null); opaque-LB gate fires z=8.46 [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp7_word_substitution_channel.json]
power: short GORILA words starve the context-hungry substitution channel
ablations_robustness: directly contrasts FND-10 (segmentation helps C/V) => channel-dependent lever
strongest_authorized_claim: Segmentation is NOT a uniform win: it hurts the substitution channel (105->47), which needs inscription-length context. The best encoding is per-channel.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That a single segmentation solves the LA obstacle.
limitations_blockers: no single best text unit across channels.
supersession_relations: corrects the WP2 'clean win' (FND-10)

canonical_name: WP8 word-segmented reduced-seed C/V bootstrap
research_question: Does the reduced-seed C/V bootstrap improve on word-segmented LA units?
method_family: semi-supervised label spreading on word-segmented units
scientific_outcome: PARTIAL_SUPPORT (coverage helped: recovers U; accuracy unvalidated on LA) / NULL validity
mechanical_verdict: opaque-LB gate unchanged (>=3 correct vowel seeds -> AUC>=0.75); LA gain = coverage not accuracy
data_sources_corpus_version: silver LA GORILA word units + DAMOS LB gate; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: WP5 bootstrap machinery; A/I typological seeds
effective_n_independence_unit: word-unit signs; unit = sign
features_channels: position + substitution on word units; A/I seeds
algorithm_model: label spreading (alpha 0.2, knn 7, beta_sub 0.5)
heldout_design: opaque-LB word-unit gate; LA vowel identity as check
positive_negative_controls: freq-only ranking AUC 0.872 (LB); supervised ceiling 0.835; unsupervised null 0.568
primary_metric_value: seeding {A,I} propagates to 6->9 vowel-like signs; word units recover U (2nd); true vowel O mis-ranked bottom (coverage not accuracy); LB min 3 correct vowel seeds -> AUC>=0.75 [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp8_word_reduced_seed_cv.json]
power: coverage improves but LA accuracy uncertain
ablations_robustness: freq-only vs clean-seed contrast retained
strongest_authorized_claim: Word units improve seed COVERAGE (recover U) but LA C/V accuracy remains unvalidated (O mis-ranked); only the opaque-LB gate is mechanically validated.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That the LA C/V partition is recovered on word units.
limitations_blockers: coverage != accuracy; true vowel O bottom-ranked.
supersession_relations: LATER REFUTED (seed-propagation = frequency artifact, task note); word-seg pair to FND-13

canonical_name: Candidate-language round 1 (West Semitic / Pre-Greek-Anatolian / agnostic control)
aliases: WS clears but control also clears
research_question: Does any of West Semitic / Pre-Greek-Anatolian beat the multi-family end-to-end null on held-out GORILA word tokens?
method_family: bounded preregistered phoneme->value correspondence + fixed cited lexicon + multi-family null
scientific_outcome: NULL (same-structure-lexicon artifact; only KU-RO='total' survives)
data_sources_corpus_version: silver LA GORILA multi-sign word tokens (973 tokens / 704 types); DAMOS LB wrong-language null; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: cited proposals (Gordon 1966; Best; Palmer; Finkelberg 2001; Woudhuizen); Ventris-Chadwick decomposition of AB syllabograms
effective_n_independence_unit: 973 held-out multi-sign tokens; effective_n predicted forms per family (e.g. ANA 233); unit = word token
features_channels: token-weighted whole-word/affix match rate
algorithm_model: fixed correspondence expansion + matcher; Holm across 3 families; leave-one-lexeme-out; 300-draw random-lexicon calibration
candidate_languages_hypotheses: West Semitic; Pre-Greek/Anatolian (Luwic); agnostic random control
preregistration_plan_hash: prereg/candidate_round1_prereg.json (registered_before_run=true, seed 20260708) — no explicit plan_hash field
heldout_design: held-out multi-sign tokens; decisive nulls = W (wrong-lang LB) + R (random-prior)
positive_negative_controls: agnostic random-lexicon control = negative; KU-RO known lexeme = the one real hit
multiplicity_correction: Holm across 3 families; deflated by identical null expansion
primary_metric_value: SEM match rate 0.04214 (Holm decisive p=0.03) but CONTROL match 0.01542 also clears raw bar (Holm 0.03) => artifact; ANA 0.00411 (Holm 0.1592); no family clears_calibrated; only KU-RO survives [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/candidate_round1_results.json]
power: adequate to detect the artifact
ablations_robustness: leave-one-lexeme-out + random-lexicon calibration
contamination_leakage: non-circular (correspondences from candidate-language dictionaries, never fitted to LA; LB only as null)
strongest_authorized_claim: No candidate family beats a random lexicon of the same shape; West Semitic's apparent significance is a same-structure artifact; only KU-RO='total' (a single known lexeme) survives.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That Linear A is West Semitic (or any tested family).
limitations_blockers: generic word-shape typicality masquerades as lexical fit.
supersession_relations: round-1 salted hash() non-determinism fixed to md5(family); replicated by FND-20/21 and FND-16
artifact_report_script_data_test_paths: scripts/candidate_round1.py; scripts/candidate_round1_wordunits.py; scripts/candidate_engine.py; prereg/candidate_round1_prereg.json; data/candidate_round1_results.json; data/candidate_wordunits.json; reports/WORDSEG_CANDLANG_SOURCES.md

canonical_name: Candidate-language round 2 (Hurrian / Luwian / control)
research_question: Do Hurrian or Luwian beat the multi-family null?
method_family: bounded preregistered correspondence + multi-family null (same engine as FND-19)
scientific_outcome: NULL
data_sources_corpus_version: silver LA 973 multi-sign tokens; DAMOS LB null; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: Wegner 2000; Wilhelm 1989 (Hurrian); Luwian sources
effective_n_independence_unit: 973 tokens; unit = word token
features_channels: token-weighted match rate
algorithm_model: fixed correspondence + Holm + loo + 300-draw calibration; deterministic md5(family) offsets
heldout_design: held-out tokens; decisive W+R nulls
positive_negative_controls: random control (did NOT clear in r2)
primary_metric_value: Hurrian match 0.03083 (Holm 1.0), Luwian 0.00206 (Holm 0.2835; only hit TA-TI), control 0.00617 (Holm 1.0); none clears [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/candidate_round2_results.json]
power: adequate
ablations_robustness: loo + calibration

canonical_name: Candidate-language round 3 (Etruscan / Aegean-isolate substrate / control)
research_question: Do Etruscan or an Aegean-isolate substrate model beat the multi-family null?
method_family: bounded preregistered correspondence + multi-family null (same engine)
scientific_outcome: NULL (the decisive clean finding: random control match rate exceeds Etruscan's)
data_sources_corpus_version: silver LA 973 multi-sign tokens; DAMOS LB null; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: Bonfante & Bonfante 2002; Facchetti; de Simone (Etruscan); Pre-Greek reconstructed from Greek loanwords (isolate)
effective_n_independence_unit: 973 tokens; unit = word token
features_channels: token-weighted match rate
algorithm_model: fixed correspondence + Holm + loo + 300-draw calibration; md5(family) offsets
heldout_design: held-out tokens; decisive W+R nulls
positive_negative_controls: random control; Aegean-isolate = zero held-out matches
primary_metric_value: Etruscan match 0.03289 (Holm 0.5374), control match 0.05447 (Holm 0.5223) EXCEEDS Etruscan; Aegean-isolate 0.0 held-out matches (Holm 1.0); none clears [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/candidate_round3_results.json]
power: the clean mechanism isolation: control bare rate 0.054 > Etruscan 0.033
ablations_robustness: isolate model predicts zero forms (falsifies 'any lexicon matches')
strongest_authorized_claim: A random lexicon of matched shape matches LA word forms as well as or better than any real language tried; nonzero LA match is generic word-shape typicality, not language-specific lexical fit.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That LA is Etruscan/Tyrrhenian or a specific isolate.
limitations_blockers: definitive on the mechanism but null on decipherment.
supersession_relations: completes the candidate-language programme; converges with FND-16

canonical_name: Sealed-prediction programme (5 hashed held-out challenge manifests)

method_family: sha256-committed held-out split manifests (pre-registration of evaluation sets)
implementation_status: DESIGNED_NOT_RUN (splits built; no candidate to score)

canonical_name: Source-lineage audit / value-lineage-independence register (63 sources)
research_question: How many sources are independent of the GORILA/Ventris VALUE lineage, and what is the load-bearing single point of failure?
method_family: source-dependency graph / lineage-cluster audit
scientific_outcome: STRUCTURAL_ONLY (honesty finding: every phonetic LA reading is single-sourced through GORILA)
data_sources_corpus_version: bibliographic audit; 25 verified on disk; as_of 2026-07-07
source_dependencies: the audit's subject IS source dependency
effective_n_independence_unit: 63 sources across 6 lineage clusters; 48 value-lineage-independent
features_channels: 6 clusters: palaeography, internal structure/metrology, archaeology, computational method, sister scripts, Ugaritic-Hebrew
algorithm_model: cluster assignment + independence-from-GORITA-value-lineage grading
heldout_design: N/A
positive_negative_controls: N/A
primary_metric_value: 63 sources audited (25 verified on disk, 6 UNAVAILABLE-with-what-they'd-add); 48 independent of GORILA/Ventris value lineage [VERIFIED_FROM_ARTIFACT data/wp7_source_register.json + SOURCE_REGISTER.csv]
power: N/A
ablations_robustness: 6 UNAVAILABLE sources documented with what they'd add (nothing fabricated)
contamination_leakage: the finding IS the leakage risk: all phonetic LA readings single-sourced through GORILA homomorphic transfer
strongest_authorized_claim: Every phonetic reading of LA is single-sourced through GORILA homomorphic transfer; only structural readings are value-agnostic — any future value claim inherits that single point of failure.
forbidden_stronger_claim: That value-independent sources establish any LA reading.
limitations_blockers: the most valuable unavailable source (Anetaki II) is forthcoming.
supersession_relations: names the acquisition target reused in SEAL_5 (FND-22)
